programme.title: REVIEW OF THE SEASON 2023/2024 (MNC)
programme.sub-title: The 2023/24 Premier League season will be forever remembered as one of the greatest campaigns ever. From an absorbing title race, Jürgen Klopp leaving Liverpool to Luton living the dream in the top-flight – Review of the Season documents all of the thrilling stories to emerge across the 380 matches.
programme.desc: The 2023/24 Premier League season will be forever remembered as one of the greatest campaigns ever. programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW EP33: LUIS GARCIA
programme.sub-title: Arriving on Merseyside in 2004 as part of a freshly recruited Spanish contingent to help build a new-look Liverpool side, Luis Garcia soon became a cult hero at Anfield. In this week’s Big Interview, Garcia shares the difficulties he faced adjusting to the Premier League, how Rafa Benitez managed to hone his talents and his thoughts on that famous “ghost” goal against Chelsea in the 2005 Champions League semi-final.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP41: (Subbed) ENZO MARESCA – THE ITALIAN JOB
programme.sub-title: A relationship steeped in glory, Chelsea Football Club is the Italian Job. Last summer, Enzo Maresca became the seventh manager from that heritage to take charge at Stamford Bridge. PL Stories looks back at the success shared between the club and managers from Italy, while also delving into Maresca’s own unique coaching journey as he strives to emulate the achievements of those who came before him.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP52: (Subbed) JURGEN KLOPP
programme.sub-title: Over the past 9 years, Jurgen Klopp has been an integral Premier League figure. With the help of former and current colleagues, fans, and friends, we look how he made the Reds a force to be reckoned with once again.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP42: (Subbed) NEURODIVERSITY CELEBRATION
programme.sub-title: In this PL Stories show, Tim Howard, who has neurodiverse conditions, leads a conversation with past and present players, exploring the challenges and strengths of living with neurodiversity. Through personal stories, including those of Joao Gomes (stutter), Steven Naismith (dyslexia), Greg Halford (autism), and Charlie Austin (ADHD), the show celebrates resilience and the positive impact neurodiverse traits bring to the football world. Tim's own journey intertwines with these stories, showcasing the triumphs that come from embracing neurodiversity.

programme.sub-title: Michael Owen burst onto the scene for Liverpool at just seventeen years of age, and in two consecutive seasons the teenage sensation won the Premier League Golden Boot. programme.title: PL STORIES EP18: DYLAN TOMBIDES
programme.sub-title: He was a star of Australia's U23 and U17 teams and made his West Ham debut at just 18 years of age, but in 2011 Dylan Tombides life was turned upside down when he received a diagnosis of testicular cancer following a routine drug test during the Under-17 FIFA World Cup. Despite a courageous three-year battle, he tragically succumbed to the illness at the age of 20 in 2014. We look back on the life of Dylan told through those who knew and loved him best, and how 10 years on since his unfortunate passing his memory still lives on through the DT38 foundation.
programme.desc: He was a star of Australia's U23 and U17 teams and made his West Ham debut at just 18 years of age, but in 2011 Dylan Tombides life was turned upside down when he received a diagnosis of testicular cancer following a routine drug test during the Under-17 FIFA World Cup. Despite a courageous three-year battle, he tragically succumbed to the illness at the age of 20 in 2014. We look back on the life of Dylan told through those who knew and loved him best, and how 10 years on since his unfortunate passing his memory still lives on through the DT38 foundation.

programme.sub-title: Nemanja Vidić grew up in war-torn Serbia and went on to become one of the greatest defenders in English football. programme.title: PL REWIND EP12: JERMAIN DEFOE: MY TOTTENHAM YEARS
programme.sub-title: Jermain Defoe scored 162 Premier League goals, and it was at Tottenham where he was at his prolific best. The striker recalls his two spells with the North London club, including the memorable occasion where he hit 5 in one match.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP34: (Subbed) CESC FABREGAS
programme.sub-title: A product of Barcelona’s famed La Masia youth academy, Cesc Fabregas is rightly remembered as one of the greatest midfield players of his generation. After making his Arsenal debut at just 16 years of age the diminutive Spaniard would go on to win titles in both England and Spain as well as becoming a World Cup winner in 2010. Now taking his first steps into coaching with Como 1907, Fabregas reflects on his footballing education as well as his hopes for the future in management.
programme.desc: A product of Barcelona’s famed La Masia youth academy, Cesc Fabregas is rightly remembered as one of the greatest midfield players of his generation. After making his Arsenal debut at just 16 years of age the diminutive Spaniard would go on to win titles in both England and Spain as well as becoming a World Cup winner in 2010. Now taking his first steps into coaching with Como 1907, Fabregas reflects on his footballing education as well as his hopes for the future in management.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP08: (Subbed) PREMIER LEAGUE MAVERICKS
programme.sub-title: We delve into the careers of some of the most enigmatic and unpredictable footballers to have graced the English top-flight in recent years and find out how they became ‘Premier League mavericks’. We get under the skin of what made these players notorious, controversial and flamboyant and the impact they had in their time in England.

programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW EP33: LUIS GARCIA
programme.sub-title: Arriving on Merseyside in 2004 as part of a freshly recruited Spanish contingent to help build a new-look Liverpool side, Luis Garcia soon became a cult hero at Anfield. But it wasn’t always plain sailing for the Catalonian. In this week’s Big Interview, Garcia shares the difficulties he faced adjusting to the Premier League, how Rafa Benitez managed to hone his talents and his thoughts on that famous “ghost” goal against Chelsea in the 2005 Champions League semi-final.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP16: (Subbed) GILBERTO SILVA
programme.sub-title: Arsenal legend and Invincible Gilberto Silva takes us back to winning the 2002 World Cup with Brazil, his early years at Arsenal and his first footballing steps in a small Brazilian town before he shines light onto the ever-famous Invincibles season with the help of Ray Parlour, Thierry Henry, Martin Keown, Patrick Vieira and none other than Arsene Wenger. Gilberto charts at all his personal and professional highs and lows.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP42: (Subbed) NEURODIVERSITY CELEBRATION
programme.sub-title: In this PL Stories show, Tim Howard, who has neurodiverse conditions, leads a conversation with past and present players, exploring the challenges and strengths of living with neurodiversity. Through personal stories, including those of Joao Gomes (stutter), Steven Naismith (dyslexia), Greg Halford (autism), and Charlie Austin (ADHD), the show celebrates resilience and the positive impact neurodiverse traits bring to the football world. Tim's own journey intertwines with these stories, showcasing the triumphs that come from embracing neurodiversity.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP41: (Subbed) ENZO MARESCA – THE ITALIAN JOB
programme.sub-title: A relationship steeped in glory, Chelsea Football Club is the Italian Job. Last summer, Enzo Maresca became the seventh manager from that heritage to take charge at Stamford Bridge. PL Stories looks back at the success shared between the club and managers from Italy, while also delving into Maresca’s own unique coaching journey as he strives to emulate the achievements of those who came before him.
programme.desc: A relationship steeped in glory, Chelsea Football Club is the Italian Job. Last summer, Enzo Maresca became the seventh manager from that heritage to take charge at Stamford Bridge. programme.title: PL STORIES EP29: ANDY ROBERTSON
programme.sub-title: From his humble beginnings at Queen’s Park in Scotland to becoming a key player for Liverpool, Andy Robertson’s rise is a tale of resilience, hard work, and determination. We delve in to his early struggles, his breakthrough at Hull City, and his crucial role in Liverpool's historic Premier League and Champions League triumphs. Featuring exclusive interviews with teammates, coaches, and Robertson himself, this episode celebrates his leadership, passion, and relentless spirit on and off the pitch.
programme.desc: From his humble beginnings at Queen’s Park in Scotland to becoming a key player for Liverpool, Andy Robertson’s rise is a tale of resilience, hard work, and determination. We delve in to his early struggles, his breakthrough at Hull City, and his crucial role in Liverpool's historic Premier League and Champions League triumphs. Featuring exclusive interviews with teammates, coaches, and Robertson himself, this episode celebrates his leadership, passion, and relentless spirit on and off the pitch.

programme.title: PL REWIND EP10: MICHAEL OWEN: MY BREAKTHROUGH YEARS
programme.sub-title: Michael Owen burst onto the scene for Liverpool at just seventeen years of age, and in two consecutive seasons the teenage sensation won the Premier League Golden Boot. Owen recalls those campaigns for the Reds, reliving the key moments that helped him develop into one of the greatest players of his generation.
programme.desc: Michael Owen burst onto the scene for Liverpool at just seventeen years of age, and in two consecutive seasons the teenage sensation won the Premier League Golden Boot. Owen recalls those campaigns for the Reds, reliving the key moments that helped him develop into one of the greatest players of his generation.

programme.title: PL REWIND EP14: SLAVEN BILIC: MY WEST HAM STORY
programme.sub-title: A West Ham cult hero, Slaven Bilic first represented the Irons as a player in the 1990s. He returned to the club as manager in 2015, ahead of the Hammers’ final season at Upton Park. The Croatian looks back on what proved to be a remarkable campaign in East London.
programme.desc: A West Ham cult hero, Slaven Bilic first represented the Irons as a player in the 1990s. He returned to the club as manager in 2015, ahead of the Hammers’ final season at Upton Park. The Croatian looks back on what proved to be a remarkable campaign in East London.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP42: (Subbed) NEURODIVERSITY CELEBRATION
programme.sub-title: In this PL Stories show, Tim Howard, who has neurodiverse conditions, leads a conversation with past and present players, exploring the challenges and strengths of living with neurodiversity. Through personal stories, including those of Joao Gomes (stutter), Steven Naismith (dyslexia), Greg Halford (autism), and Charlie Austin (ADHD), the show celebrates resilience and the positive impact neurodiverse traits bring to the football world. Tim's own journey intertwines with these stories, showcasing the triumphs that come from embracing neurodiversity.
programme.desc: In this PL Stories show, Tim Howard, who has neurodiverse conditions, leads a conversation with past and present players, exploring the challenges and strengths of living with neurodiversity. Through personal stories, including those of Joao Gomes (stutter), Steven Naismith (dyslexia), Greg Halford (autism), and Charlie Austin (ADHD), the show celebrates resilience and the positive impact neurodiverse traits bring to the football world. Tim's own journey intertwines with these stories, showcasing the triumphs that come from embracing neurodiversity.

programme.sub-title: Mario Melchiot recalls his Premier League journey, which began as part of an ambitious Chelsea team. The Dutch defender played in the Blues’ high-stakes final day clash in 2003 with Liverpool, before moving to Birmingham City and then captaining Wigan Athletic.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP41: (Subbed) ENZO MARESCA – THE ITALIAN JOB
programme.sub-title: A relationship steeped in glory, Chelsea Football Club is the Italian Job. Last summer, Enzo Maresca became the seventh manager from that heritage to take charge at Stamford Bridge. PL Stories looks back at the success shared between the club and managers from Italy, while also delving into Maresca’s own unique coaching journey as he strives to emulate the achievements of those who came before him.
programme.desc: A relationship steeped in glory, Chelsea Football Club is the Italian Job. Last summer, Enzo Maresca became the seventh manager from that heritage to take charge at Stamford Bridge. PL Stories looks back at the success shared between the club and managers from Italy, while also delving into Maresca’s own unique coaching journey as he strives to emulate the achievements of those who came before him.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP39: (Subbed) ARSENE WENGER AND HIS MANAGERIAL JOURNEY
programme.sub-title: We explore Arsène Wenger's managerial journey before joining Arsenal, tracing his evolution from a young, innovative coach in France to a transformative force in European football. Delving into his time at Nancy, AS Monaco and Nagoya Grampus Eight, Wenger's tactical brilliance began to shape his future success. We highlight the key moments that led him to one of football's most iconic roles.
programme.desc: We explore Arsène Wenger's managerial journey before joining Arsenal, tracing his evolution from a young, innovative coach in France to a transformative force in European football. Delving into his time at Nancy, AS Monaco and Nagoya Grampus Eight, Wenger's tactical brilliance began to shape his future success. We highlight the key moments that led him to one of football's most iconic roles.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP43: (Subbed) EDUARDO DA SILVA
programme.sub-title: Eduardo da Silva will forever be synonymous for suffering one of the worst injuries in Premier League history. We travelled to Brazil, Croatia and caught up with the former Arsenal hero here in England to find out more about the man who gave Gunners fans a fleeting glimpse of greatness before being so cruelly deprived.
programme.desc: Eduardo da Silva will forever be synonymous for suffering one of the worst injuries in Premier League history. We travelled to Brazil, Croatia and caught up with the former Arsenal hero here in England to find out more about the man who gave Gunners fans a fleeting glimpse of greatness before being so cruelly deprived.

programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW EP34: ANDONI IRAOLA
programme.sub-title: Having arrived on these shores to manage Bournemouth in 2023, Andoni Iraola took over a club which had narrowly avoided relegation. Today, they are considered one of the toughest sides to play against in the Premier League. With exclusive access, this week the Bournemouth manager gives the Big Interview a guided tour of some of the local spots he likes to visit on the Dorset coastline. Iraola opens up on growing up in Spain, playing alongside Xabi Alonso and Mikel Arteta, how his footballing philosophy is turning Bournemouth’s fortunes around and what he loves most about the place he now calls home.
programme.desc: Having arrived on these shores to manage Bournemouth in 2023, Andoni Iraola took over a club which had narrowly avoided relegation. Today, they are considered one of the toughest sides to play against in the Premier League. With exclusive access, this week the Bournemouth manager gives the Big Interview a guided tour of some of the local spots he likes to visit on the Dorset coastline. Iraola opens up on growing up in Spain, playing alongside Xabi Alonso and Mikel Arteta, how his footballing philosophy is turning Bournemouth’s fortunes around and what he loves most about the place he now calls home.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP32: (Subbed) MANCHESTER CITY - THE SEASON THAT STARTED IT ALL
programme.sub-title: In a nail-biting climax to the 2011-2012 Premier League season, Manchester City clinched the title with unparalleled drama. Facing Queens Park Rangers on the final matchday, City secured a 3-2 victory with Sergio Agüero's iconic last-minute goal, securing their first league title in 44 years and etching that game into footballing folklore. They have since become the dominant power in English football and we look at how it all began.
programme.desc: In a nail-biting climax to the 2011-2012 Premier League season, Manchester City clinched the title with unparalleled drama. Facing Queens Park Rangers on the final matchday, City secured a 3-2 victory with Sergio Agüero's iconic last-minute goal, securing their first league title in 44 years and etching that game into footballing folklore. They have since become the dominant power in English football and we look at how it all began.
